q: Equilibrium means a state of ___.
There are two types of equilibrium; ___ equilibrium and dynamic equilibrium.
Consider a book at rest on a table. Both the forces are equal in magnitude but opposite in direction, resulting in ___ equilibrium.
All chemical reactions are ___.
In a sealed bottle of soda, ___ is present in both the liquid phase and the gaseous phase.
The maintenance of equilibrium in reactions between ___ and oxygen in human blood is one of the processes involving human health.
The ___ cycle contributes to the important equilibrium process that minimises the impacts of carbon dioxide on the environment.
A chemical equilibrium is a ___ equilibrium.
In a dynamic equilibrium, reactants are continuously being changed to products and products are continuously being changed back to ___.
A dynamic equilibrium exists once a ___ reaction occurs.
The products cannot be converted back to reactants in many chemical reactions that go to ___.
There is the ___ reaction where reactants are converted into products.
There is the ___ reaction where the products are converted back into the starting reactants.
The symbol ___ is used to represent the reversible reaction.
A reversible reaction is one that can occur in both the forward and ___ directions.
The conversion of reactants to products and the conversion of products to reactants occur ___.
Reversible reactions that happen in a ___ system eventually reach equilibrium.
When the ___ of the forward and reverse reactions balance, the reversible reaction is in equilibrium.
If a reversible reaction is in a closed system, the system can reach a state called a ___ equilibrium.
In dynamic equilibrium, the forward and reverse reactions do not ___; rather, they continue to occur at the same rate.
Dynamic equilibrium leads to ___ concentrations of the reactants and products.
Generally, the reaction begins with only the concentrations of reactants and there are no ___ formed.
As the reaction proceeds towards equilibrium, the concentrations of reactants gradually ___.
As the reaction proceeds towards equilibrium, the concentrations of products gradually ___.
After a system has reached equilibrium, the concentrations of all substances remain ___.
Initially, the rate of the forward reaction is ___ and the rate of the reverse reaction is low.
The equilibrium is reached when the two ___ are equal.
In equilibrium, the forward and reverse reactions are still proceeding at an ___ rate.
Based on the phase of the reactants and products, chemical equilibrium can be classified into homogeneous and ___ chemical equilibria.
In homogeneous chemical equilibrium, the reactants and the products of chemical equilibrium are all in a ___ phase.
In heterogeneous chemical equilibrium, the reactants and the products of chemical equilibrium are present in ___ phase.
A ___ system is a system that allows energy to enter and exit, but matter can neither be gained nor lost.
Le Chatelier's principle is a ___ guide to the effect of changes in concentration, temperature, and pressure on a system at equilibrium.
When one of the factors (stress) changes, the ___ of chemical equilibrium is effectively altered.
Le Chatelier's idea was discovered by French Chemist ___ in 1884.
If a system is in equilibrium and any of the factors affecting the chemical equilibrium is changed, the equilibrium ___ so as to cancel out the effect of the change.
By adding something, the system will react to ___ it.
By removing something, the system will react to ___ it.
The greater the ___, the more the direction of the reaction shifts in response to the stress.
A ___ does not affect the equilibrium concentrations and there is no change in equilibrium.
Adding or removing a reactant or product from a reaction mixture at equilibrium will always ___ the equilibrium.
When the concentration of a reactant is increased, the reaction proceeds in the ___ direction in favour of products.
When the concentration of a reactant is decreased, the reaction proceeds in the ___ direction in favour of reactants.
The effect of temperature on chemical equilibrium depends upon the ___ of the reaction.
If the forward reaction is exothermic, the reverse reaction will be ___.
A temperature ___ favours an exothermic reaction.
A temperature ___ favours an endothermic reaction.
In the case of exothermic reactions, equilibrium shifts towards the ___ reaction by releasing heat.
In the case of endothermic reactions, equilibrium shifts towards the ___ reaction by absorbing heat.
Pressure changes do not markedly affect the ___ or liquid phases.
The pressure of a gas mixture at a given temperature is determined by the number of ___ in a given volume.
If the pressure is increased, the position of equilibrium will shift towards the side with ___ moles of gas.
According to Le Chatelier's principle, as pressure increases the equilibrium moves toward the side of the reaction with ___ moles of gas.
As pressure decreases, the equilibrium moves toward the side of the reaction with ___ moles of gas.
If the number of moles of gas is the same on both sides of the reversible chemical equation, pressure has ___.
Industrial processes are usually designed to give the maximum possible ___ of products.
The synthesis of ammonia (Haber process) is a good example of an industrial process that uses ___ principle.
Since the Haber process reaction is ___, the forward reaction is favoured at low temperatures.
The catalyst used in ammonia production is ___ with a small amount of aluminium oxide.
Methanol is an alcohol that is used in fuels, making methyl esters, and creating ___ resin glues.
Methanol is produced from ___ and hydrogen.
The methanol production reaction is carried out at a temperature of $250 \, ^{\circ}C$ using the catalyst ___.
In 1864, two Norwegian chemists proposed the ___ as a general description of the equilibrium condition.
The law of mass action is a ___ law for predicting the amounts of reactants and products.
According to the law of mass action, for a reversible reaction in a closed system, the ___ between the concentration of reactants and products is constant.
The rate of the forward reaction is directly ___ to the concentration of the reactants raised to a power equal to their coefficients.
The ratio $k_{f} / k_{r}$ is called the ___ constant.
The equilibrium constant $K_{eq}$ is a characteristic ___ value of the ratio of product concentrations to reactant concentrations.
The value of $K_{eq}$ is constant only at a specified ___.
The units of equilibrium constant depend on the form of the ___.
Pure solids or liquids are ___ from the equilibrium expression because their concentrations remain constant throughout the reaction.
If the $K_{eq}$ value is large, the equilibrium lies to the ___ and there are more products than reactants.
If the $K_{eq}$ value is small, the equilibrium lies to the ___ and there are more reactants than products.
If the $K_{eq}$ value is equal to ___, both reactants and products are equally favoured at equilibrium.
If molar concentrations are used, the equilibrium constant can be expressed as ___.
For equilibrium that involves gases, ___ can be used instead of concentration.
The equilibrium constant ___ can be given as the ratio of the partial pressures of products and reactants.
Because ___ is a pure liquid, its concentration is constant at a given temperature.
The equilibrium for the ___ of iodine crystals is an example of the solid-vapour heterogeneous equilibrium state.
The numerical value of the equilibrium constant is determined by measuring the ___ of each substance involved in a chemical reaction at equilibrium.
Equilibrium constant is a ratio that expresses the ___ between the amounts of products and reactants present at equilibrium.
When running on a treadmill, the person is in constant motion or ___ equilibrium.
